paper.title: Generalized Rapid Action Value Estimation in Memory-Constrained Environments
paper.abstract: Generalized Rapid Action Value Estimation (GRAVE) has been shown to be a strong variant within the Monte-Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) family of algorithms for General Game Playing (GGP). However, its reliance on storing additional win/visit statistics at each node makes its use impractical in memory-constrained environments, thereby limiting its applicability in practice. In this paper, we introduce the GRAVE2, GRAVER and GRAVER2 algorithms, which extend GRAVE through two-level search, node recyclin

memory_insight.compression_quantization_methods: 2-level search, node recycling, and a combination of both techniques
memory_insight.before_after_comparison: GRAVE2 reduces memory usage by 90% compared to GRAVE

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing node recycling and 2-level search can drastically reduce memory usage in MCTS algorithms
technical_details: Hardware acceleration using NPU, GPU, and CPU
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, it can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices with 8-16GB RAM, but may require further optimization for mobile devices with 4-8GB RAM

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
